update_available: There's a newer Plinky ready when you are.
update_broken: Updates can’t be installed on this device right now — the app keeps working, and reloading later will retry.
library_intro: Songs, scales and arpeggios, studies, and anything you've brought along yourself — all in one place. Filter by kind or grade, star what you're working on, and open one whenever you'd like to read, hear, or play it.
meta_library_description: Your piano scores on this device — search, star, and practise every piece, and bring your own MusicXML along.
home_library_blurb: Thousands of pieces — scales and arpeggios, studies, and tunes you'll recognise — plus anything you bring along. Star your favourites, filter by grade, and open one whenever you'd like to read, hear, or play it note by note.
meta_home_title: Plinky — piano practice in your browser
meta_home_description: Practise piano in your browser with a MIDI keyboard or your computer keyboard — sight-reading, rhythm, tempo, ear training, and loop drills, with your scores kept on your device.
meta_play_description_fallback: Play a piece with your MIDI piano or computer keyboard
meta_play_description: Play "{title}" in your browser — sight-read it on your MIDI or computer keyboard.
meta_play_description_by: Play "{title}" by {composer} in your browser — sight-read it on your MIDI or computer keyboard.
meta_daily_description: Today's piece — the same one for everyone — graded and shareable
you_intro: How you're getting on — your grade and skill, what's ready for you next, and how it's been going.
meta_you_description: Your grade, skill, and practice progress on plinky
grades_skill_help: Rises as you play harder pieces well — no ceiling, just keep climbing.
refresh_why: Pieces resurface on a widening schedule, so what you've learned actually sticks.
grades_all_fresh: All fresh — nothing to review right now.
meta_review_description: Refresh the pieces that are fading, one at a time
grades_competitive_help: Pieces you stop practising fade from your grade until you refresh them.
grades_start_hand: Set your hand size for finger positions that fit
discover_intro: Here's how Plinky works: pick anything that catches your eye, Practice walks you through it note by note, and every finished run is graded. Set yourself up first if you like — but nothing's locked, and there's no rush.
discover_ear: Train your ear in any piece
discover_fingering: Work out the finger positions in any piece
discover_compose: Record a tune of your own in Compose
discover_import: Bring a score of your own
discover_midi: Connect your MIDI piano — or let Plinky listen through the microphone — in Settings
grade_about_1: Five-finger tunes that stay in one hand position — steady quarter and half notes, nothing to stretch for.
grade_about_2: A little hand movement and simple two-hand coordination; mostly steps with the odd small skip.
grade_about_3: Both hands working together more, slightly wider reaches, and the first dotted rhythms and eighth-note runs.
grade_about_4: Position shifts, simple chords, and phrases that ask each hand to do something of its own.
grade_about_5: Faster passages, fuller chords, and accidentals that pull you out of the home key.
grade_about_6: Independent hands throughout, broader leaps, and ornaments or syncopation to hold in time.
grade_about_7: Demanding two-hand textures, quick position changes, and dense rhythms at a real tempo.
grade_about_8: Concert-level pieces — rapid runs, big stretches, and the full range of the keyboard.
help_intro: How Plinky works, area by area — have a look wherever you're curious.
help_empty: Help for this area is on the way.
meta_help_description: How Plinky works: guides for every part of the app, from your first note to tracking your progress.
board_intro: Pianists and composers we love right now — hand-picked by the Plinky team. Follow them, soak up some inspiration, and the piano will be here when you get back.
board_empty: Nothing is pinned to the board yet — check back soon.
home_board_blurb: Artists we love right now — hand-picked, worth a follow.
meta_board_description: The artists Plinky is listening to right now: hand-picked pianists and composers worth following.
storage_write_failed: This device's storage is full or blocked — new progress isn't being saved.
sound_ios_hint: No sound? On iPhone, turn off Silent Mode (the side switch, or the Action button on newer models) and turn the volume up.
sound_inapp_hint: No sound here? This in-app browser blocks it — tap the ••• menu and choose Open in Safari.
coach_play_modes: A piece has two tabs: Play holds the score — with ear training (Hidden notes) and the fingering editor inside full screen — and Runs keeps your saved performances. Switch any time.
coach_practice_loop: New here? Have a listen first, then play along as slowly as you like. If you slip, keep going — there's no need to start over.
transpose_caption: Changes the notes you play, not just the view
keep_up_hint: The score advances on the beat at a fixed tempo, and any note you don't catch in time slips by. A one-bar count-in leads you in.
guide_notes_hint: Each note sounds as it passes, so you can follow along. Turn it off to read them at tempo yourself.
duet_hint: The app plays the hand you're not practising, in time — so you play a duet with it.
keep_up_result: You kept up with {inTime} of {total} notes in time.
race_ghost_hint: A translucent replay of your best run on this piece plays alongside you.
rotate_hint: Turn your phone sideways for a bigger keyboard.
export_print_hint: Sheet music on paper — or save it as a PDF.
export_midi_hint: The notes as sound — for GarageBand, a DAW, or another practice app.
export_musicxml_hint: Editable notation — for MuseScore and other score editors.
loop_hint_narrow: tap two bars on the score to loop just a passage
tempo_trainer_caption: Nudges the tempo up a notch after each finished run, so you build speed a little at a time.
tempo_trainer_target_caption: The top speed the trainer climbs to before it stops nudging.
metronome_subdivision_caption: Clicks per beat — 1 on the beat, 2 for eighths, 3 for triplets, 4 for sixteenths.
hand_caption: Take a two-hand piece one hand at a time, or play both together.
loop_caption: Repeats the whole piece. For just a passage, tap two bars on the score (they turn red) — the range and controls appear right by the score.
fingering_show_mine_caption: Draws the fingering you saved for this piece onto the staff.
bars_per_row_caption: Forces this many bars onto each row for bigger notation; Auto fits them to your screen.
treadmill_hint: The piece reads as one line that scrolls as you play, instead of stacked rows.
highway_hint: The notes to come stack above the keys and fall onto them as you play, so you can see which key is next.
highway_label: Upcoming notes falling onto the keys
bar_numbers_hint: Numbers the first bar of each row, so a passage is easy to find. Turn it off for a cleaner staff.
beams_caption: Beams join fast notes into beat groups. Hide them to read one note at a time while you're learning; Auto shows them on harder pieces, where the grouping helps.
note_size_caption: Magnifies the whole score for bigger, easier-to-read notes — handy on a small screen, or when you're starting out.
color_notes_hint: Each notehead takes a colour from its name — every C red, every D orange, and so on — so you can read by hue while the names sink in.
reading_level_help: Sets the reading aids together — pick where you are, then fine-tune any of them below.
reading_level_custom_help: Your own mix of aids. Pick a level to reset them.
settings_reading_hint: How the music reads while you play, and the aids that help you find the next note.
hand_size_instruction: tap your thumb key, then your pinky — or play them on a MIDI piano
home_eyebrow: Free · in your browser · nothing to install
home_keyboard_hint: Tap a key — that's a plink.
home_intro: Free, in-browser piano practice — no account, nothing to install. Bring a MIDI keyboard or use your computer keys, then play any score you like, or drill sight-reading, rhythm, tempo, and ear training. Every run is graded, and your scores and progress stay on your device.
arcade_blurb: Fresh scales and arpeggios that climb as you clear them — read at sight and keep going.
perf_label: Each note placed by timing, ringed in red if the key was wrong, dashed if you hesitated
hands_even: Your hands kept pace with each other.
hand_left_lagged: Your left hand lagged a little behind your right.
hand_right_lagged: Your right hand lagged a little behind your left.
share_grid_label: How you played across six moments of the run, one row per hand
settings_mastery_threshold: Mark a score learned at grade
settings_mastery_help: A score counts as learned once a run reaches this grade, which schedules it for review. Lower the bar to mark scores learned sooner.
settings_decay_gentle_help: Your grades never drop — pieces just lose their shine until you refresh them. The kind default.
settings_decay_competitive_help: Neglected pieces can lapse, so a grade — and your skill rating — can actually slip. For bragging rights: a ⚔️ badge shows you're playing this way.
settings_review_cap_help: How many due pieces a review session offers in one go, so it stays a finishable batch rather than a pile.
play_compose_blurb: Improvise freely and Plinky catches every note, sketching your playing onto a staff you can share or take away as MusicXML or MIDI.
meta_compose_description: Improvise at the keyboard and Plinky sketches the notation — share your take or download it as MIDI or MusicXML
compose_intro: Play freely on a MIDI keyboard, your computer keys, or the keys below — Plinky catches every note and sketches it onto the staff. Count in to play along to the metronome for tidier notation, or set a checkpoint to keep the good part and retry the rest. Your take is yours to share or download, and a MIDI or MusicXML file opens right back up where you left off.
compose_staff_label: A staff sketch of your improvisation
compose_staff_empty: Play a few notes and they'll appear here.
compose_recording_idle: Recording — play anything, and every note is caught
compose_sketch_note: The audio is exactly what you played; the staff is an approximate sketch, snapped to a grid so it reads as notation.
compose_open_error: Couldn't read that file — a MIDI (.mid) or MusicXML (.musicxml, .mxl) file should open fine.
daily_intro: A new piece every day — the same one for everyone, a piano take on the daily puzzle. Give it a go, see how it lands, and share your grid if you'd like.
settings_subtitle: Your preferences stay on this device.
settings_show_fingerings: Show finger position hints while practising
settings_note_hints: Show the next note to play
settings_note_hints_help: A wrong key always flashes red. This sets when the correct key lights up — keeping it hidden leaves the reading to you.
settings_note_labels_help: Note names printed on the keys while you learn where they live. "Just C" marks only the C keys — the white key left of each pair of black keys — as a landmark to orient by.
settings_hand_size_hint: Tell Plinky how far you reach and the suggested finger positions follow your hands. With one hand, just set that one.
settings_get_help: Get help or report a bug
settings_privacy_hint: Your call — these stay off unless you turn them on.
settings_analytics_desc: Share anonymous usage analytics (Google Analytics) so we can see which features get used and make Plinky better. It loads only while this is on, never sends anything that identifies you, and you can switch it off any time.
settings_reset_help: Erases everything on this device — your scores, progress, and settings. This can't be undone, so grab a backup from the Library first if you'd like to keep your scores.
settings_reset_failed: Some data couldn't be erased — this device's storage may be full or blocked. Nothing was reloaded; try again.
meta_assignments_description: Build a practice assignment — an ordered list of pieces and exercises — and share it as a file or a link
assignments_intro: An assignment is an ordered list of pieces and exercises, each with a target tempo and note if you want one. Build one and share it as a file or a link — whoever opens it gets it as a track, and their progress stays on their own device.
assignments_received_heading: An assignment was shared with you
assignments_received_detail: "{name}" — {count} items. Import it to add it to your tracks.
assignments_search_placeholder: Search pieces and exercises to add…
assignments_reorder_hint: Drag a title to reorder, or use the arrow buttons.
assignments_empty_basket: Nothing here yet — search above, and what you add builds up the list.
assignments_starter_description: A gentle first set: two familiar tunes, then three short studies.
assignments_save_hint: An assignment is ready to save once it has a name and at least one piece.
assignments_yours_empty: No assignments yet — build one above, or bring in a file or link.
assignments_save_failed: Couldn't save — this device's storage may be full.
assignments_link_copied: Share link copied to the clipboard.
assignments_available_count: {available} of {total} pieces are available on this device.
assignments_imported_partial: Imported "{name}" — {available} of {total} pieces are available on this device.
home_assignments_blurb: A sequence of pieces — a teacher's, or one you build yourself — as a guided path, with each step graded as you clear it.
progress_grid_label: Accuracy, timing and flow across your recent sessions
import_intro: Got a score of your own? Drop the MusicXML in and it renders, plays, and grades like any other piece. Most notation apps — MuseScore, Finale, Sibelius, Flat — export MusicXML.
import_local_note: Scores you add stay on this device and are never uploaded. Clearing your browser data removes them, so
import_drop_here: Drop a MusicXML file here, or click to browse
takes_empty_hint: Play a piece through and the run is kept here for you — to replay, to race its ghost, or to challenge a friend with.
takes_save_failed: Couldn't save this run — this device's storage is full or blocked.
takes_share_ghost: Challenge a friend with this run
midi_badge_disconnected: No piano connected — these keys are yours to play
ghost_track_label: Race: you at note {you} of {total}, ghost at note {ghost}
sprint_intro: Sight-reading drills with fresh notes every run — read the phrase at sight, play it on your keyboard, and see how it grades for accuracy, timing, and flow.
play_not_found: That score isn't on this device.
play_unavailable: Can't load this piece right now — check your connection and try again.
fingering_color_legend: As you go: green flows easily, amber works, red is an awkward reach.
focus_strip_label: The bars you're playing now, above the keyboard
fingering_empty: Nothing to finger in this passage — the other hand or the next bars will have more.
keyboard_hint_no_piano: No piano? Your computer keyboard plays just fine — or Plinky can listen to a real piano through the microphone:
keyboard_hint_shift: shift both hands an octave — current offset
keymap_help: No piano? These keys play the notes. Tap a note, then press the key you'd like it on — or keep the standard layout with Reset.
keymap_press_pedal: Press a key for this pedal (Space works), or Backspace to clear
keymap_pedals_help: Bind a key to each pedal — Space suits the sustain pedal. Tap a pedal, then press a key (or Backspace to clear).
keymap_pedal_taken: That key already plays a note — pick a free one.
import_duplicate: This piece is already in your library — adding it again just points to the same one.
import_no_notes: No playable notes in there — is this valid MusicXML?
import_read_error: That file couldn't be read — try choosing it again.
import_save_failed: Couldn't save the score — it may be larger than this device's storage allows.
score_load_error: This score couldn't be displayed — the MusicXML may be malformed.
backup_intro: Your scores live only on this device — {count} so far. A backup is yours to download any time, and a bundle you've saved (or one a teacher shared) comes straight back in.
takes_download_video_portrait: Save a portrait video (9:16, for Reels and Shorts)
person_empty: Nobody by that name in the catalogue yet.
meta_person_description: {name}’s pieces on Plinky — free sheet music to play in your browser.
hidden_notes_hint: Play by ear: the notes start hidden and reveal as you find them — green when you get one, red when the tries run out. Have a listen first, then play back what you heard.
reveal_tries_caption: Wrong attempts a hidden note allows before it reveals itself in red.
fingering_heat_legend: Shaded bars are harder to finger — the deeper the red, the trickier the passage.
run_group_skill_hint: Tell Plinky where you're at and the reading aids below follow. Tune any one and it reads Custom.
run_group_practice_hint: Pick a hand to focus on, and whether the score waits for you or holds a steady tempo.
run_group_reading_hint: A little help reading the notes — colour them by pitch, see them coming, or hide them to test your ear.
run_group_layout_hint: How the sheet music is drawn, and how it moves along as you play.
run_group_challenge_hint: A few ways to stretch yourself once a piece feels comfortable.
forgiving_hint: A missed note no longer stops you — play the next one and the score moves on.
metronome_toggle_hint: A steady click to play along to. In Keep up it always ticks.
scroll_follow_hint: The score scrolls to keep the note you're on in view. Turn it off to move the sheet yourself.
finger_numbers_hint: Print the suggested finger for each note on the staff.
metronome_accent: Accent the first beat of each bar
settings_appearance_hint: How Plinky looks, and which language it speaks.
settings_sound_hint: Plinky can play every note you press. Press Test to hear one!
settings_mastery_hint: How well a song has to go before it counts as learned.
settings_grades_hint: What happens to your grades when you take a break.
settings_fingering_hint: Little helpers that show which key and finger to use.
settings_metronome_hint: The steady click that helps you keep time.
settings_midi_hint: Plug in a real piano keyboard, and Plinky can hear you play.
settings_labels_example: Try it! This little piano shows your choice.
achievements_hint: Everything you've earned. Badges are yours forever — taking a break never removes one.
mic_hint: No cable needed — Plinky can listen through the microphone while you play your own piano.
mic_listening: Listening — one note at a time works best.
mic_denied: Microphone access was declined. Allow it in your browser to play this way.
mic_play_something: Play a key on your piano…
mic_calibrate_lead: A quick tune-up, so Plinky hears your piano clearly.
mic_calibrate_saved: Your tuning is saved on this device.
mic_calibrate_quiet: Shh… listening to your room. Stay quiet for a moment.
mic_calibrate_note: Now play this note on your piano:
mic_calibrate_soft: Now play any note as softly as you can.
mic_calibrate_loud: Now play a note firmly — like you really mean it.
mic_calibrate_done: All set! Plinky is tuned to your piano.
meta_ear_description: Name what you hear — intervals and single notes, straight in your browser. No piano, no sign-up, no cost.
home_ear_blurb: Away from the piano? Your ears can still practise — name the notes and the distances between them.
ear_intro: No piano needed here — just your ears and a quiet minute.
ear_level_help: Each one keeps the intervals before it and adds a few more.
ear_intervals_blurb: Two notes, one after the other. The rungs sit at the distance they name, so an answer has a height as well as a word.
ear_pitch_blurb: One note on its own, no clues — the keyboard is where you name it.
ear_verdict_close: Not that one — green is what played.
ear_session_recorded: That's a round done — it counts toward your grades.
ear_chords_blurb: A chord, all its notes at once. Name the quality you hear — the colour a chord has whatever its root.
ear_scales_blurb: A scale climbing from its tonic. Name which one it is by the pattern of steps.
ear_progressions_blurb: A run of chords in a key. Name each one you hear, in order — it starts and ends on the home chord, so the key is always there to hold onto.
ear_scale_degrees_blurb: A short cadence sets the key, then one note. Name where it sits in the key — the first degree, the third, the fifth?
ear_intervals_context_blurb: A cadence sets the key, then two notes. Name the interval between them, heard with the key to lean on.
ear_melodic_blurb: A cadence sets the key, then a little tune. Write it down degree by degree, in order.
legal_mt_notice_body: This page was written in German. Only the German version is legally binding — maßgeblich ist die deutsche Fassung. This machine translation is provided purely as a convenience for readers who do not speak German. If anything here differs from the German original, the German text applies.
datenschutz_controller_intro: The controller responsible for data processing on this website is:
datenschutz_short_body: Plinky is a browser-only application. Your settings, your progress and your recordings are stored only locally in your browser; we do not collect them or transmit them to a server. There are no user accounts and no advertising. By default there is no analytics and no tracking. Only if you explicitly enable it in the settings is an anonymous usage analysis (Google Analytics) loaded — and you can switch it off again at any time.
datenschutz_hosting_body: The website is delivered as static files via GitHub Pages (GitHub, Inc., USA). When the page is requested, the host processes technically necessary data such as your IP address, the date and time of access and the amount of data transferred, in order to deliver the page and keep operations secure. The legal basis is our legitimate interest in secure and stable delivery (Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR). Any transfer to the USA relies on the provider's certification under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework.
datenschutz_localstorage_body: To run the app we use your browser's local storage — for example for your settings, your practice progress and saved recordings. This data stays on your device, is not transmitted to us, and can be deleted at any time via the settings or your browser. The legal basis is Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR (providing a functional, convenient app).
datenschutz_fonts_body: Fonts are loaded from our own server. No external services such as Google Fonts are used, so no data is transmitted to third parties in this respect.
datenschutz_thirdparty_body: A notice banner with current news may appear on the home page. Its contents are loaded on request from the content service Sanity (Sanity.io, Denmark, EU). For technical reasons your IP address is transmitted to the provider in order to deliver the content. The legal basis is Art. 6(1)(f) GDPR. If the request fails, the app continues to work unchanged.
datenschutz_analytics_body: If — and only if — you enable the anonymous usage analysis in the settings, we embed Google Analytics 4 (provider: Google Ireland Limited, Gordon House, Barrow Street, Dublin 4, Ireland). It helps us understand which features are used, so we can improve Plinky. Anonymised usage data is processed in the course of this; Google Analytics 4 does not store full IP addresses. The legal basis is your consent under Art. 6(1)(a) GDPR and § 25(1) TDDDG. You can withdraw your consent at any time with effect for the future by switching the analysis off again in the settings. A transfer to the USA may take place; it relies on Google's certification under the EU-US Data Privacy Framework. You can find more information in Google's privacy policy.
datenschutz_email_body: If you write to us by email, we process the information you provide solely in order to handle your enquiry (Art. 6(1)(b) or (f) GDPR).
datenschutz_rights_body: You have the following rights with regard to your personal data: access, rectification, erasure, restriction of processing, objection to processing, and data portability. You also have the right to lodge a complaint with a data protection supervisory authority. To exercise your rights, please contact us at the address given above.
consent_banner_body: May we use Google Analytics to see which features get used and make Plinky better? It sets cookies. Nothing loads unless you agree, and you can change your mind any time in Settings.
